name: view_foundry_object_type
title: view_foundry_object_type
description:
View an existing object type.

This tool allows you to view the definition of an object type in the ontology, including its properties, link types, and action types.

This tool can be used repeatedly/recursively to traverse the Ontology graph, by looking at its LinkTypes.
You can offer this option to the user when they are asking vague details about ontology entities, as traversing the ontology can provide clarity on its structure and potential uses.

anyOf.description:
The global branch rid: e.g. ri.branch..branch.{UUID}.
Must be provided if available objects within a branch are not visible on other branches or in the main ontology

globalBranchRid.description:
The global branch rid: e.g. ri.branch..branch.{UUID}.
Must be provided if available objects within a branch are not visible on other branches or in the main ontology

objectTypeRid.description: Ontology Object Type RID. (eg: ri.ontology.main.object-type.{UUID}). undefined.

objectTypeId.description:
This is a human readable id for the ObjectType. Valid ObjectTypeIds have the following conditions:
* Preferably kebab-case (but snake_case and cameCase is also allowed)
* The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
* No special characters are allowed.

name: create_or_update_foundry_object_type
title: create_or_update_foundry_object_type
description:
Create or update Object type in the Ontology.
An object type is a schema definition of a real world entity or event - similar to a database table.
When you are working with an Object Type, you will be dealing with either an individual object (an instance) or a collection of objects of the same type.
You can optionally specify a folder path or RID (via the location field) to save the object type into a specific folder or project.
For more information reference the documentation.

globalBranchRid.description:
The global branch rid: e.g. ri.branch..branch.{UUID}.
Must be provided if available objects within a branch are not visible on other branches or in the main ontology

displayName.description: What you want to call the newly created object type

apiName.description:
A string indicating the API Name to use for the given ObjectType. This API name will be used to access the
ObjectType in programming languages. Valid API names have the following conditions:
  * PascalCase
  * The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
  * No special characters are allowed.

objectTypeId.description:
This is a human readable id for the ObjectType. Valid ObjectTypeIds have the following conditions:
* Preferably kebab-case (but snake_case and cameCase is also allowed)
* The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
* No special characters are allowed.

propertyTypeId.description:
PropertyTypeId:
This is a human readable id for the PropertyType. Valid ids.PropertyTypeIds have the following conditions:
  * Preferably snake_case (but kebab-case and cameCase is also allowed)
  * The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
  * No special characters are allowed.

description.description: A short text that describes the PropertyType.

displayName.description: A string that would be used as the name of the PropertyType just for display purposes.

displayMetadata.description: This includes metadata which can be used by front-ends when displaying the PropertyType.

isLongText.description:
This flag can be set if the PropertyType is known to contain large texts. This can be used to
determine how to index it and how it should be rendered in the front-end.

supportsExactMatching.description:
This flag can be set if you need to support efficient wildcard queries on the PropertyType.
The performance improvements will be especially significant if the property contains high cardinality values.
The text being searched is not tokenized.

type.description: Wrapper type for the various supported property types. e.g { type: 'integer' }, { type: 'string', string: { isLongText: false } }

indexedForSearch.description:
A flag to control if the PropertyType should be indexed for search. This can be set to false for
properties that do not need to be searchable or aggregatable.

apiName.description:
A string indicating the API Name to use for the given entity that will be a field of an ObjectType.
This API name will be used to access the entity in programming languages. Valid API names have the following conditions:
  * cameCase
  * The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
  * No special characters are allowed.

isNullable.description: A flag which indicates if the property is nullable

propertyTypes.description: A PropertyTypeModification this will dictate what properties the object type will have, please ensure propertyTypeId is unique. If modificationType is UPDATE missing properties from the list will be deleted

primaryKey.description: PropertyType to be used as the primary key

titlePropertyTypeId.description: PropertyType id used for the title of the object in the UI

folderPath.description: The path to a Foundry folder or project (e.g. /namespace/project_name/folder)

location.description: The Foundry folder or project to create the type in. Can be specified as a path or a RID. This field accepts both folderRids and projectRids interchangeably.

modificationType.description: Specify modification type for the Ontology.

anyOf.description: Defines the property as edit only (primary key cannot be editOnly); data will not be stored in the datsource; instead it will be stored and managed in the Ontology

mappingInfo.description: Wrapper type for mapping info e.g { type: 'column', column: 'exampleColumn'}, { type: 'editOnly' }

backingDataset.description:
The dataset that will back data for the object 
- An object may have more properties than there are columns in the dataset. Properties not backed by a dataset will be stored in the ontology and updated by users via APIs
- The dataset has to contain a primary key for the object-type

iconName.description: A BlueprintJS icon name for this object type. Common examples: "person", "office", "document", "database", "globe", "briefcase", "truck", "shopping-cart", "build", "timeline-events". Any valid BlueprintJS icon name can be used.

iconColor.description: Hex color for the icon, e.g. "#4C90F0"

icon.description: Optional icon for the object type. If not provided, defaults to a blue cube icon.

description.description: Optional description for the object type. Describes the real-world entity or event this object type represents.

name: create_or_update_foundry_link_type
title: create_or_update_foundry_link_type
description:
Create or Update a Link Type in the Ontology. This is required when you want to define a relationship between two different Object Types in your Ontology.
If the modificationType is set to CREATE, we will create a new link. You can optionally specify a folder path or RID (via the location field) to save the link type into a specific folder or project.
If the modificationType is set to UPDATE, we will update an existing link.
If you are asked to "modify", "update" or "change" a link, this usually means the link already exists and you should set the modification type to UPDATE.

Each Link Type requires two Object Types - so every link has two "sides".
The sides are referred to by different names, depending on what type of link is being created. Specifically:

- ONE_TO_MANY (1<>n):
    - this is the most common type of link type.
    - For example, a "Supplier" Object Type may have a 1<>n Link Type to a "Supplier Address" Object Type.
    - In this example, a supplier can have many addresses, but an address can only ever belong to one supplier.
    - left side: "Supplier"
    - right side: "Supplier Address"

- ONE_TO_ONE (1<>1):
    - this is less common, but still used.
    - For example, a "User" object type might have a 1<>1 link to "User Profile".
    - In this example, a user can only ever have one user profile, and a user profile can only belong to one user.

- MANY_TO_MANY (n<>n):
    - this is less common, but still used.
    - For example, a "Student" object type might have a n<>n link to "Course".
    - In this example, a student can be enrolled in many courses, and a course can have many students enrolled.

globalBranchRid.description:
The global branch rid: e.g. ri.branch..branch.{UUID}.
Must be provided if available objects within a branch are not visible on other branches or in the main ontology

folderPath.description: The path to a Foundry folder or project (e.g. /namespace/project_name/folder)

location.description: The Foundry folder or project to create the type in. Can be specified as a path or a RID. This field accepts both folderRids and projectRids interchangeably.

modificationType.description: Specify modification type for the Ontology.

displayName.description: What you want to call the newly created link type. This should be in Title Case. e.g. 'Supplier Address'

pluralDisplayName.description: plural form of displayName. This should be in Title Case. e.g. 'Supplier Addresses'

apiName.description: The API name for the link type. This should be in camelCase. e.g. 'supplierAddress'

linkTypeId.description:
This is a human readable id for the LinkType. Valid LinkTypeIds have the following conditions:
* Preferably kebab-case (but snake_case and cameCase is also allowed)
* The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
* No special characters are allowed.
* 
* Example: "my-link-type"
* 
* It's good convention for the link type to clearly describe the relationship between the two object types.
* For example, if the link-type is between a "book" and an "author", the link type could be "book-to-author-link".

linkTypeCardinality.description: The cardinality of the link type.

objectTypeId.description:
This is a human readable id for the ObjectType. Valid ObjectTypeIds have the following conditions:
* Preferably kebab-case (but snake_case and cameCase is also allowed)
* The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
* No special characters are allowed.

propertyId.description:
PropertyTypeId:
This is a human readable id for the PropertyType. Valid ids.PropertyTypeIds have the following conditions:
  * Preferably snake_case (but kebab-case and cameCase is also allowed)
  * The first character must be a lower case letter, subsequent characters can be a lower case letter or number.
  * No special characters are allowed.

leftSide.description:
The LEFT side of the LinkType. In a One-to-Many Link, this is the 'one' side. 
The property ID must always be the PrimaryKey of the LEFT-side object type.

rightSide.description:
The RIGHT side of the LinkType. 
* In a One-to-One or a Many-to-Many Link, the property ID must be the PrimaryKey of the RIGHT-side object type.
* In a One-to-Many Link, the property ID must be a ForeignKey in the RIGHT-side object type that points to the PrimaryKey of the LEFT-side object type.
###
Example:
If One "Supplier" has Many "Supplier Addresses", the left side is the "Supplier" object type and the right side is the "Supplier Address" object type.
The primary key of the "Supplier" object type (i.e. left) would likely be something like "supplierId" or "supplierCode".
In order for a One-to-Many link to be set up for this example successfully, the property ID of the right side must be a ForeignKey in the "Supplier Address" 
object type that points to the PrimaryKey of the "Supplier" object type, which would likely be a property with a name like "supplierId"

displayName.description: The display name for the left-to-right link. For ONE_TO_MANY links, this describes how to get from the 'one' object to its 'many' objects.

pluralDisplayName.description: The plural display name for the left-to-right link

apiName.description: The API name for the left-to-right link

displayName.description:
The display name for the right-to-left link. 
For ONE_TO_MANY links, this describes how to get from a "many" object to its "one" object.

pluralDisplayName.description: The plural display name for the right-to-left link

apiName.description: The API name for the right-to-left link

manyToManyLinkTypeDatasources.description: The dataset and primary key mappings for the link type. This is only required for Many-to-Many links.
